sectionOrderRules: Hero must state the firm's expertise, target client or problem area, and the primary CTA without vague management filler.
Services overview should appear before long company story so visitors can quickly confirm relevance.
Selected cases preview should come before deep about copy because this industry sells on proof as much as on credentials.
Trust signals must stay factual, such as case outcomes, client types, sectors or years of experience, without invented certifications or exaggerated counts.
Contact CTA should appear by the end of the page with a clear next step such as request a quote or start a conversation.
Services list must be grouped by real offer areas or assignment types, not abstract buzzwords.
Ideal client fit should clarify when the service is suitable and what kinds of needs the firm typically solves.
If pricing is mentioned, present it as approach, scope logic or starting point only when truthful; do not imply fixed packages unless they exist.
FAQ should answer practical purchase questions and reduce friction before contact.
Each case card should name the assignment type or problem solved and summarise the delivered value in concrete language.
Do not overclaim measurable outcomes if exact figures are unavailable; describe improvements honestly.
Case method note can explain confidentiality limits when client work cannot be shown in full detail.
About story should explain background, specialisation and why the business exists in practical terms.
Expertise highlights should focus on domain knowledge, methods, sectors or problem areas rather than generic passion statements.
Team block may collapse to founder profile when the company is a solo specialist.
Process steps should describe the actual way of working from first dialogue to delivery and follow-up.
Contact info must include direct and usable contact methods such as email and phone when available.
Contact form should ask for the essentials needed to understand the assignment without making the form unnecessarily long.
Inquiry guidance should help the visitor describe scope, timeline or challenge to improve lead quality.
